uploaderPublicationDecision.reason: Uploader publication is owner-approved, hosted Release Check evidence is recorded, npm registry readback returns @agentique.io/uploader@0.2.0, and clean install smoke from the npm registry package passes. The package remains review-only and does not publish, approve, certify, host, or moderate resources.

parserVariantPublicationDecision.reason: Parser/variant package changes are published in the coordinated 0.2.0 package release. Hosted Release Check passed on main, GitHub Actions trusted publishing completed, registry readback confirms all companion packages at 0.2.0, registry install smoke passed, and the public rollback/unpublish procedure is documented.

canonicalTicketPatchCandidate.reason: The canonical-host POST ticket flow and live envelope compatibility are source-revision patch changes after the published 0.2.0 package set. The coordinated 0.2.1 patch candidate keeps companion package versions aligned; the behavior changes are concentrated in readback and uploader.

catalogDownloadPublicationDecision.reason: Owner approval is recorded to execute the coordinated 0.2.1 patch publication through the checked-in GitHub Actions Trusted Publishing workflow. The release remains scoped to package availability for catalog/download helper changes; direct broad byte-transfer availability, resource approval, safety certification, installation, extraction, and execution claims remain excluded. Post-publish registry readback, clean install smoke, rollback evidence, and branch cleanup must be recorded before the closeout evidence is complete.

agentNativePublicationDecision.reason: Owner approval is recorded to execute the coordinated 0.2.1 patch publication through the checked-in GitHub Actions Trusted Publishing workflow. The release remains scoped to package availability for agent-native schemas, validator findings, readback/badge helpers, uploader dry-runs, and starter metadata; resolver availability, direct install, credential handling, runtime compatibility, approval, publication of resources, and safety claims remain excluded. Post-publish registry readback, clean install smoke, rollback evidence, and branch cleanup must be recorded before the closeout evidence is complete.
